mitigations.mitigation_name: Harden the Ubuntu host's egress DNS behavior by forcing all DNS resolution through the internal firewall/resolver and blocking direct external DNS queries from the host

args.command: ip route
ls -l /etc/resolv.conf && cat /etc/resolv.conf && ls -l /run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf 2>/dev/null || true
printf 'nameserver 192.168.1.1\noptions timeout:1 attempts:2\n' > /etc/resolv.conf && chmod 644 /etc/resolv.conf && cat /etc/resolv.conf

python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('/etc/resolv.conf')
p.write_text('nameserver 192.168.1.1\noptions timeout:1 attempts:2\n')
p.chmod(0o644)
print(p.read_text(), end='')
PY
getent ahosts google.com | head -3 && ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo LAN_PING_OK && wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo HTTP_OK
iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp --dport 8853 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT && iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp --dport 8853 -d 203.0.113.1 -j REJECT && iptables -L OUTPUT -n --line-numbers | sed -n '1,12p'

ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo LAN_PING_OK; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo HTTP_OK; (echo >/dev/udp/203.0.113.1/8853) >/dev/null 2>&1; echo DNS8853_TEST_DONE

mitigations.mitigation_name: Disable or tightly restrict access to high-risk attacker utilities on the compromised Ubuntu host, such as dnsutils, curl, nmap, and hping3, while preserving ping and wget

args.command: chmod 000 /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/nmap /usr/bin/hping3 2>/dev/null; chmod 755 /usr/bin/wget /usr/bin/ping 2>/dev/null; ls -l /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/nmap /usr/bin/hping3 /usr/bin/wget /usr/bin/ping
command -v hping3; command -v curl; command -v dig; command -v nslookup; command -v nmap; command -v wget; command -v ping
chmod 000 /usr/sbin/hping3 /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/nmap; ls -l /usr/sbin/hping3 /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/nmap /usr/bin/wget /usr/bin/ping

chmod 000 /usr/sbin/hping3 /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/nmap; ls -l /usr/sbin/hping3 /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/nmap /usr/bin/ping /usr/bin/wget; command -v hping3; command -v curl; command -v dig; command -v nslookup; command -v nmap; command -v ping; command -v wget

mitigations.mitigation_name: Harden local file and directory permissions on sensitive paths so the unprivileged attacker account cannot read candidate staging or data collection locations

args.command: id && ls -ld /usr/share/pixmaps /usr/share/icons /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16 /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64 2>/dev/null && stat -c '%a %U %G %n' /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png 2>/dev/null

chmod 0640 /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png && chown root:root /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png && stat -c '%a %U %G %n' /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png && ls -l /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png
find /usr/bin /bin /usr/local/bin -maxdepth 1 \( -name dig -o -name nslookup -o -name host -o -name curl -o -name wget \) -type f -exec ls -l {} \;
chmod 000 /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/host /usr/bin/curl && stat -c '%a %U %G %n' /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/host /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/wget
id agent 2>/dev/null || true; ls -l /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/host /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/wget

mitigations.mitigation_name: Restrict outbound DNS from the Ubuntu host to the approved recursive resolver only, preventing direct DNS tunneling to external infrastructure while preserving ICMP and HTTP validation traffic

args.command: nft list ruleset
ip route

nft add table inet dns_guard; nft 'add chain inet dns_guard output { type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept; }'; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 53 ip daddr != 192.168.1.1 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 53 ip daddr != 192.168.1.1 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 8853 ip daddr != 192.168.1.1 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 8853 ip daddr != 192.168.1.1 drop; nft list ruleset

dig @203.0.113.1 -p 8853 test.example.com +time=1 +tries=1; echo EXIT:$?; dig @192.168.1.1 test.example.com +time=1 +tries=1; echo EXIT2:$?; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo WGET:$?

nft delete table inet dns_guard; nft add table inet dns_guard; nft 'add chain inet dns_guard output { type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept; }'; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 53 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 53 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 8853 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 8853 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 53 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 53 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 8853 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 8853 drop; nft list ruleset

nft insert rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 53 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft insert rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 53 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft insert rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 8853 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft insert rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 8853 ip daddr 192.168.1.1 accept; nft list ruleset
nft delete table inet dns_guard

nft add table inet dns_guard; nft 'add chain inet dns_guard output { type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept; }'; nft add rule inet dns_guard output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 53 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 53 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 8853 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 8853 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output udp dport 8853 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output tcp dport 8853 drop; nft list ruleset

dig @192.168.1.1 test.example.com +time=1 +tries=1; echo DIG_LOCAL:$?; dig @203.0.113.1 -p 8853 test.example.com +time=1 +tries=1; echo DIG_EXT:$?; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo WGET:$?; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2; echo PING:$?

nft delete table inet dns_guard; nft add table inet dns_guard; nft 'add chain inet dns_guard output { type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept; }'; nft add rule inet dns_guard output oifname "eth0" ip daddr 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output oifname "eth0" ip daddr 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 accept; nft add rule inet dns_guard output oifname "eth1" udp dport 53 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output oifname "eth1" tcp dport 53 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output oifname "eth1" udp dport 8853 drop; nft add rule inet dns_guard output oifname "eth1" tcp dport 8853 drop; nft list ruleset
